title: Orientation: What Happens Inside One Transformer Block

cues.label: Hidden-state rows enter
cues.headline: Hidden-state matrix enters the block
cues.narration: The block receives one row per token. It will preserve the row count while changing what each row represents.

cues.label: Next-token evidence
cues.headline: Rows become evidence for next-token logits
cues.narration: The row matters because later layers and the output head use it to change next-token scores.

cues.label: Shape contract
cues.headline: The block is specific edits to the same table
cues.narration: Attention, MLP, residuals, and LayerNorm are different edits to the same hidden-state table.

cues.label: Q and K compare
cues.headline: Queries and keys create attention scores
cues.narration: Q asks what a token is looking for. K advertises what each token can match. Their dot products become scores.

cues.label: Q and K compare
cues.headline: Vector alignment is the mechanism
cues.narration: The human labels are analogy. The actual mechanism is learned vector alignment.

cues.label: Softmax and V mix
cues.headline: Scaling keeps softmax from becoming too sharp
cues.narration: Scaling keeps score magnitudes calm enough for softmax to make graded choices.

cues.label: Softmax and V mix
cues.headline: Weights choose value content
cues.narration: Softmax creates weights. Those weights pull content from V rows.

cues.label: Misleading shortcut
cues.headline: Q/K/V are learned projections, not tokenizer facts
cues.narration: Q asks, K matches, V carries. The weights route, but V carries the content.

cues.label: Residual add
cues.headline: Attention updates the stream without erasing it
cues.narration: The attention output becomes a delta added back into the original residual stream.

cues.label: Notebook metaphor
cues.headline: The residual stream is a shared notebook
cues.narration: The stream keeps the old signal while each block writes useful updates into it.

cues.label: MLP update
cues.headline: The MLP edits each row independently
cues.narration: The MLP transforms each row after context has arrived: expand, gate, compress, add.

cues.label: MLP update
cues.headline: Expansion gives the row room for intermediate features
cues.narration: The wider hidden space gives the MLP room to create and gate candidate features.

cues.label: Next-token evidence
cues.headline: Better rows make better next-token scores
cues.narration: Blocks refine hidden states so the output head can assign better vocabulary logits later.

cues.label: Attention heads
cues.headline: Multiple heads read the same table in different ways
cues.narration: Each head has its own Q, K, and V projections and contributes one reading of the same table.

cues.label: Residual add
cues.headline: LayerNorm creates a stable read path
cues.narration: The sublayer reads a normalized view while the residual path carries the continuing stream.

cues.label: Misleading shortcut
cues.headline: Fix the wrong shortcut version
cues.narration: The corrected chain is projection, scoring, weighting, value mixing, residual add, MLP update, later logits.

cues.label: Next-token evidence
cues.headline: Wrong predictions become inspectable
cues.narration: Once you know the chain, a mistake can be traced to representations, scores, weights, updates, or logits.

cues.label: Q and K compare
cues.headline: The visual should track the changing object
cues.narration: The visual should show the real object being discussed: score grids, value mixing, residual adds, or MLP updates.
